blurb: Delivers government information to Albertans through news, digital channels, and publications, coordinates public consultations and engagement on policy decisions, manages the alberta.ca digital platform, and administers freedom of information and privacy protection for public bodies.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Albertans are informed about government decisions, services, and emergencies
goals.description: The ministry ensures Albertans receive timely, accurate, and accessible information about government decisions, programs, services, and emergencies through news releases, digital channels, publications, and media relations.

foundational_services.name: Set government communications policy and standards
foundational_services.desc: Develops and enforces communications policy, advertising standards, election communication rules, social media policy, and alternative communications policy to ensure coordinated, ethical, and accessible government messaging across all ministries.

supporting.name: Read government news releases and announcements
supporting.desc: The Government of Alberta publishes news releases, statements, and announcements on alberta.ca/news. Citizens can browse by ministry, topic, or date to stay informed about government decisions, policy changes, funding announcements, and program updates.

reach_estimate.basis: Primary news channel for all government announcements reaching the full Alberta population

economic_impact.note: Informed citizens can access programs, comply with regulations, and participate in democratic governance

supporting.name: Subscribe to government news and topic alerts
supporting.desc: Albertans can subscribe to email alerts for government news filtered by ministry, topic, or region. Subscribers receive notifications when new releases, announcements, or policy updates are published.
supporting.users: Citizens, media, stakeholders wanting proactive updates

reach_estimate.basis: Email subscriptions for government news across all topics and ministries

economic_impact.note: Proactive notification ensures Albertans learn about relevant programs and deadlines

supporting.name: Receive emergency alerts and critical safety information
supporting.desc: The Government of Alberta issues emergency alerts for wildfires, severe weather, public safety threats, and other critical events through the Alberta Emergency Alert system. Alerts are distributed through mobile devices, broadcast media, and online channels.

reach_estimate.basis: Wireless emergency alerts reach all mobile devices in affected areas

economic_impact.note: Timely emergency alerts save lives and enable evacuation and safety response

main_services.name: Find government information and services online

supporting.name: Search for government services and information
supporting.desc: Alberta.ca is the Government of Alberta's central website, providing access to all ministry services, programs, forms, and information. CPE manages the platform's content standards, information architecture, search functionality, and accessibility.
supporting.users: All Albertans seeking government services or information

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of millions of visits annually
reach_estimate.basis: Central digital front door for all Government of Alberta services

economic_impact.note: Primary channel through which Albertans discover and access all government services

supporting.name: Access government publications, reports, and data
supporting.desc: The Open Government portal and alberta.ca provide access to government publications including annual reports, business plans, budget documents, datasets, and research reports across all ministries.

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of downloads and views annually
reach_estimate.basis: Open data portal and publications serve researchers, media, and engaged citizens

economic_impact.note: Open data supports research, journalism, innovation, and democratic accountability

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Albertans can participate in government decisions that affect them
goals.description: The ministry coordinates public engagement and consultation processes across government, giving Albertans opportunities to provide input on policies, programs, legislation, and infrastructure projects before decisions are made.

foundational_services.name: Develop public engagement policy, methods, and quality standards
foundational_services.desc: Establishes standards and best practices for public engagement across government, including engagement frameworks, evaluation criteria, accessibility requirements, and reporting on how public input influenced decisions.

main_services.name: Participate in a public consultation or engagement

supporting.name: Find and join an open public consultation
supporting.desc: Albertans can search for open public consultations and engagement opportunities across all government ministries. Topics range from policy development and legislative changes to infrastructure projects and program design. Each engagement explains the issue, timeline, and how to participate.
supporting.users: All Albertans interested in contributing to government decisions

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of participants annually across all engagements
reach_estimate.basis: Multiple concurrent consultations on major policy and infrastructure decisions

economic_impact.note: Public input improves policy decisions and builds social licence for government action

supporting.name: Submit feedback on a proposed policy, regulation, or project
supporting.desc: Albertans can submit written feedback, complete surveys, or participate in online discussions on proposed policies, regulations, and projects. Submissions are reviewed and considered in the decision-making process, with summary reports published afterward.
supporting.users: Citizens, stakeholders, industry, organizations with views on government proposals

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of submissions per major consultation
reach_estimate.basis: High-profile consultations on energy, health, education draw significant public response

economic_impact.note: Structured feedback channels ensure diverse perspectives inform policy development

supporting.name: View results and outcomes of past public engagements
supporting.desc: After a public engagement closes, the government publishes summary reports showing what was heard, how many people participated, and how public input influenced the final decision. This closes the feedback loop and builds public trust.
supporting.users: Past participants, researchers, media, general public

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of report views annually
reach_estimate.basis: Published outcomes for all formal government engagements

economic_impact.note: Transparency about how input was used maintains public trust and future participation

main_services.name: Contact the government or your elected representative

supporting.name: Send a message to the Premier or a minister
supporting.desc: Albertans can write directly to the Premier or any cabinet minister to share views, raise concerns, or request information. Contact forms, mailing addresses, and email contacts are published for all ministers.

channels.label: Office of the Premier mailing address

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of messages annually
reach_estimate.basis: Premier and minister offices receive substantial public correspondence

economic_impact.note: Direct communication channel between citizens and elected officials supports democratic accountability

supporting.name: Find contact information for a government office or service
supporting.desc: Alberta.ca provides a directory of government offices, ministry contacts, and service-specific phone numbers and email addresses. Albertans can find the right contact for any government program or inquiry.
supporting.users: All Albertans needing to reach a specific government office

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of directory lookups and calls annually
reach_estimate.basis: Central contact point for navigating all government services

economic_impact.note: Efficient wayfinding reduces citizen frustration and connects people to the right service faster

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Government information is accessible and privacy is protected
goals.description: The ministry administers freedom of information and privacy protection legislation, ensuring Albertans can access government records, that personal information held by public bodies is protected, and that government operates with transparency and accountability.

foundational_services.name: Administer the freedom of information and privacy protection framework
foundational_services.desc: Oversees the implementation of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act across all public bodies, develops policies and guidelines, coordinates with the Information and Privacy Commissioner, and ensures public bodies comply with access and privacy obligations.
foundational_services.users: Government FOIP coordinators, Information and Privacy Commissioner

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act

supporting.name: Submit a freedom of information request
supporting.desc: Any person can submit a formal access request under the FOIP Act to obtain records held by a Government of Alberta public body. Requests are processed within legislated timelines, with fees for search and copying. Certain records may be withheld under specific exemptions.

channels.label: Mail request to public body FOIP coordinator

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of FOIP requests annually across all public bodies
reach_estimate.basis: Established demand from media, researchers, and citizens seeking government records

economic_impact.note: Freedom of information is a cornerstone of democratic accountability and public trust

supporting.name: Appeal a decision on an access request
supporting.desc: If a FOIP request is denied, partially granted, or if fees seem unreasonable, the requester can ask the Information and Privacy Commissioner to review the decision. The Commissioner can order disclosure or recommend changes.
supporting.users: FOIP requesters dissatisfied with a public body's decision

channels.label: Request for review to the Commissioner
Office of the Information and Privacy Commissioner

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act

reach_estimate.basis: Subset of FOIP requesters who challenge decisions

economic_impact.note: Independent review ensures public bodies apply exemptions appropriately

main_services.name: Protect your personal information held by government

supporting.name: Request your own personal information from a public body
supporting.desc: Albertans have the right under FOIP to access their own personal information held by government public bodies. This includes records related to benefits, licensing, enforcement actions, and any other files where the individual's information appears.
supporting.users: Individuals seeking their own records from government

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of personal information requests annually
reach_estimate.basis: Individuals regularly request their own government-held records

economic_impact.note: Personal access rights empower individuals to verify and correct government-held information

supporting.name: File a privacy complaint about how government handled your information
supporting.desc: If an Albertan believes a public body has improperly collected, used, or disclosed their personal information, they can file a complaint with the Information and Privacy Commissioner. The Commissioner investigates and can order corrective action.
supporting.users: Individuals concerned about government handling of their personal information

channels.label: Office of the Information and Privacy Commissioner

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act

reach_estimate.basis: Growing privacy awareness drives complaint volume

economic_impact.note: Complaint mechanism enforces privacy standards and deters careless information handling

supporting.name: Learn about your privacy rights and how government uses personal information
supporting.desc: The government publishes privacy impact assessments, collection notices, and plain-language guides explaining how personal information is collected, used, and protected by public bodies under FOIP. Albertans can learn their rights and what to expect.
supporting.users: All Albertans, privacy advocates, legal professionals

legislation.full_name: Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of page views and guide downloads
reach_estimate.basis: Growing public interest in personal data protection and privacy rights

economic_impact.note: Privacy literacy helps citizens protect themselves and hold government accountable
